items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda document does not specify the project details. Residents may want to review the full meeting minutes or staff report to understand what construction work is being approved and where it will occur.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project gets consultant contract extension
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to extend the consultant agreement with DKS Associates for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This infrastructure project aims to improve safety and traffic flow at the railroad crossing on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: City awards contract for local street pavement replacement project
items.summary: The council approved bids and awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project (10626). This project will resurface multiple local streets across San José.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Active Transportation Plan with revised criteria
items.summary: The city presented Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan on April 7, including new scoring criteria and edits to guide future bike and pedestrian infrastructure projects. This plan will shape how Cupertino prioritizes walking and cycling improvements across the city.

items.headline: City Council Vacancy Process Changes Under Consideration
items.summary: The council is considering an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled and reduce the number of vacancies. This could affect how future open council seats are handled in Milpitas.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium will build a nearly $9M senior housing development with 65 units, addressing San José's shortage of affordable senior living options. This represents significant new housing supply for an underserved demographic in the city.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition suing city over Mary Avenue safety
items.summary: The city is defending against litigation filed by the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns in Santa Clara Superior Court (Case No. 26CV488611). This suggests an ongoing dispute about traffic or pedestrian safety on a local street.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pickup becomes annual San José city event
items.summary: The city council approved making the Great American Litter Pick-up an annual city-sponsored special event on March 25, 2026. The city will provide funding and can accept donations of materials and services for the cleanup effort.
